title: [InstCombine] Do not fold `shufflevector(select)` if the select condition is a vector
summary: The generalization strategy involved expanding a foldable pattern from a single scalar condition case to handle vector conditions, while preserving safety constraints. Initially, the optimization only applied to `select` with a scalar condition (`i1`), folding a shuffle into the select's operands. The patch removed this restriction, enabling the fold for vector selects as long as the condition is a vector of integers (not elementwise). This allows the transformation to apply to the new test cases: `shuf_same_length_vec_select` (vector condition with same-length shuffle) and `shuf_larger_length_vec_select` (vector condition with larger shuffle). The key insight is that integer vector conditions still represent a per-lane selection, making the shuffle's lane rearrangement safe to push through. Developers should identify similar overly restrictive type checks and evaluate whether the core operation semantics (here, lane-wise selection) remain valid under broader conditions.
